title: A New Friend at School
summary: Two girls meet for the first time at school and discover they have things in common.

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: Hi! My name is Lily. Are you new here?

body.speaker: Student2
body.text: Yes, I am! My name is Grace. I started today.

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: Welcome to our school! Where are you from?

body.speaker: Student2
body.text: I am from London. My family moved here last month.

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: That is so cool! Do you like our school so far?

body.speaker: Student2
body.text: Yes, I do! Everyone is very nice. But I do not know where the library is.

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: Oh, I can show you! It is on the second floor, next to the science room.

body.speaker: Student2
body.text: Thank you so much! Do you like reading?

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: Yes, I love reading! My favourite books are about magic and adventures.

body.speaker: Student2
body.text: Me too! I love magic stories. Maybe we can go to the library together.

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: Yes, let us go after lunch today!

body.speaker: Student2
body.text: Great! I am so happy I have a new friend already.

body.speaker: Student1
body.text: Me too! We are going to be great friends, I think.

annotations.exampleEn: He looked lost because he was new here.
She started today, so she did not know anyone yet.
His family moved to a new city last year.

annotations.meaningVi: cho đến nay / đến bây giờ
annotations.exampleEn: How do you like the new school so far?
She returned the book to the library.
The art room is on the second floor.
He loves books about adventures in far-off lands.
Let us play outside after school.
She had already finished her homework.
They became great friends after one week.
We do experiments in the science room.

grammarNotes.title: Let us / Let's for suggestions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Let's' (viết tắt của 'let us') dùng để đề nghị làm gì đó cùng nhau: 'Let us go after lunch today!' Đây là cách dùng phổ biến nhất trong tiếng Anh thông thường.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Let's' (short for 'let us') is used to make a suggestion to do something together: 'Let us go after lunch today!' This is the most common form in everyday English.

grammarNotes.title: Simple present for likes and dislikes
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng simple present để nói về những điều bạn thích hay không thích: 'I love reading.' / 'I do not know where the library is.' Thêm 's' vào động từ với he/she/it: 'She loves reading.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the simple present to talk about likes and dislikes: 'I love reading.' / 'I do not know where the library is.' Add 's' to the verb with he/she/it: 'She loves reading.'

translationVi: Student1: Xin chào! Tên mình là Lily. Bạn có phải là người mới đến đây không? Student2: Đúng vậy! Tên mình là Grace. Mình mới bắt đầu hôm nay. Student1: Chào mừng đến trường của chúng mình! Bạn đến từ đâu? Student2: Mình đến từ London. Gia đình mình chuyển đến đây tháng trước. Student1: Thật tuyệt! Đến nay bạn có thích trường mình không? Student2: Có, mình thích! Mọi người rất tốt bụng. Nhưng mình không biết thư viện ở đâu. Student1: Ồ, mình có thể chỉ cho bạn! Nó ở tầng hai, cạnh phòng khoa học. Student2: Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều! Bạn có thích đọc sách không? Student1: Có, mình rất thích đọc sách! Những cuốn sách yêu thích của mình là về phép thuật và phiêu lưu. Student2: Mình cũng vậy! Mình thích những câu chuyện về phép thuật. Có lẽ chúng mình có thể cùng đến thư viện. Student1: Vâng, chúng mình hãy đến sau bữa trưa hôm nay! Student2: Tuyệt vời! Mình rất vui vì đã có một người bạn mới rồi. Student1: Mình cũng vậy! Mình nghĩ chúng mình sẽ là những người bạn thân thiết.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the new student?
questions.explanation: 'My name is Grace. I started today.'
questions.hint: Listen for the new student's introduction.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the student who already goes to the school?
questions.hint: Find the first line of the dialogue.
Look for the sentence about Grace's hometown.

questions.prompt: What does Grace say she cannot find?
questions.explanation: 'I do not know where the library is.'
'It is on the second floor, next to the science room.'
questions.hint: Find Lily's directions to the library.

questions.prompt: What is next to the library?
questions.explanation: 'It is on the second floor, next to the science room.'
questions.hint: Look at the directions Lily gives.

questions.prompt: What kind of books does Lily love?
questions.explanation: 'My favourite books are about magic and adventures.'
questions.hint: Find Lily's sentence about favourite books.

questions.prompt: Does Grace share Lily's love of magic stories?
questions.hint: Find Grace's reaction to Lily's favourite books.

questions.prompt: When do the girls plan to go to the library?
questions.explanation: 'Yes, let us go after lunch today!'
questions.hint: Find the sentence with a time plan.

questions.prompt: How does Grace feel about making a new friend?
questions.explanation: 'I am so happy I have a new friend already.'

questions.prompt: What does Lily think about their friendship?
options: She thinks they will be great friends
She wants to think about it
She is too busy for friends
questions.explanation: 'We are going to be great friends, I think.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Grace is new at school and does not know many people yet. What are some good ways to help a new student feel welcome and comfortable in a new school?
